info.name: FyndRX API
info.description:
Complete API collection for FyndRX e-Pharmacy Platform

**Authentication:**
- **Public Browsing Endpoints** (API key only):
  • Drug search & viewing
  • Pharmacy search & prices
  • Reviews (viewing only)
  • Forms (viewing only)
  • Recent/Top searched drugs
  • Blog posts
  • Authentication endpoints
  • Consultations (create, stats, by type)
  • Stats & Analytics (platform, search, user behavior, revenue, content, pharmacy/drug performance, real-time)

- **Authenticated Endpoints** (Bearer token required):
  • User profile & settings
  • Cart operations
  • Orders
  • Prescriptions (user-specific)
  • Consultations (full CRUD + actions)
  • Reviews (create/update/delete)
  • Favorites (drugs & pharmacies - add, remove, list, check, bulk operations)
  • Saved drugs (legacy endpoints)
  • Recently viewed (user-specific)
  • Notifications

**Setup:**
1. Set API_KEY in your .env file
2. Set the api_key variable in Postman collection
3. Login to get access_token for authenticated endpoints

**Pagination:**
Most list endpoints now support pagination with 'page' and 'per_page' query parameters. Default per_page is 15. All paginated responses follow the format:
```json
{
  "data": [...],
  "meta": {
    "current_page": 1,
    "per_page": 15,
    "total": 100,
    "last_page": 7,
    "from": 1,
    "to": 15
  }
}
```

**Stats & Analytics:**
All stats endpoints support date range filtering with 'period' parameter (today, week, month, year, last_7_days, last_30_days, last_90_days, all) or custom range with 'from' and 'to' date parameters (YYYY-MM-DD format).

**Favorites:**
Favorites endpoints allow users to save drugs and pharmacies for quick access. Drugs are stored in saved_drugs table, pharmacies are stored in user's favorite_pharmacies JSON column. Supports bulk operations for efficiency.

variable.description: API key for public endpoints (set in .env as API_KEY)
Rotating refresh token for renewing access tokens
Set after creating or listing a consultation
UUID of MUDI CHEMIST — from seeded data
UUID of MUDI CHEMIST - OSU branch — from seeded data
UUID of PANADOL TABLETS 500mg — from seeded data
UUID of Panadol 500mg price at Mudi Chemist — from seeded data
UUID of PrimeCare Drugstore draft application — from seeded data
UUID of blog post — from seeded data
Slug of first seeded blog post

item.name: ⚙️ Platform Settings & Status
item.description:
Endpoints for retrieving global platform configuration, such as maintenance mode status, application versions, and global delivery fees. 

**Note**: These endpoints are heavily cached and highly available, making them safe to poll on application boot.

request.description:
### 🌐 Platform Status Check

Retrieves the current public configuration for the FyndRX platform. This includes critical flags like `maintenance_mode`.

**Rate Limit**: `120 requests/minute` (api:public throttle group).

#### 💡 Pro-tip for Frontend:
Call this endpoint when the app boots up. If `maintenance_mode` is `true`, immediately overlay a maintenance screen using the provided `maintenance_message` and block further API calls to save bandwidth.

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Endpoints for user registration, authentication, and security management.

request.description:
# 📱 Send Registration OTP

Initiate the registration or password reset process by sending an OTP to the user's phone.

### Usage
Call this endpoint first when a user wants to sign up or reset their password.

### Required Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phone_number` | string | The user's mobile number. Formats like `024...` or `+233...` are accepted. |

### Optional Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `email` | string | Backup email address. |
| `reset_password` | boolean | Set to `true` if this is for a password reset flow. Checks if user exists. |

# ✅ Verify OTP

Verify the OTP sent to the user's phone.

### Usage
Call this after the user enters the code they received.

### Required Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phone_number` | string | The number the OTP was sent to. |
| `otp` | string | The 4-6 digit code received. |

# 📝 Complete Registration

Create a new user account after successful phone verification.

### Usage
Submit this form to create the account. Requires the valid OTP again for security confirmation.

### Required Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `firstname` | string | User's first name. |
| `lastname` | string | User's last name. |
| `email` | email | Unique email address. |
| `phone_number` | string | Unique phone number. |
| `password` | string | Min 8 characters. |
| `otp` | string | The valid OTP code for final verification. |

# 🔓 Login

Authenticate a user and retrieve an access token.

### Auto-Configuration
> **Note:** This request automatically sets the `access_token` collection variable upon success. You don't need to copy-paste it manually!

### Inputs
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `login` | string | **Required**. Either an **Email** or **Phone Number**. |
| `password` | string | **Required**. User's password. |

# 🔐 Reset Password

Set a new password for the user.

### Flow
1. Call `Send OTP` with `reset_password: true`.
2. User receives code.
3. Call this endpoint with the code and new password.

### Required Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phone_number` | string | User's phone. |
| `otp` | string | Verification code. |
| `password` | string | New password (min 8 chars). |
| `password_confirmation` | string | Must match `password`. |

# ⚠️ Delete Account

Permanently delete a user account.

### Warning
This action is irreversible. All user data, including order history and profiles, will be removed or anonymized.

### Requirements
User must provide their password to confirm deletion.

# 👋 Logout

Invalidate the current access token.

### Headers
Requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

# 🧪 Test Registration

**DEVELOPMENT ONLY**

Skip SMS verification to quickly create a test user.

# 🔄 Refresh Access Token

Request a new short-lived access token using a rotating refresh token.

### Web (SPA) Flow
For browsers, the `refresh_token` is sent automatically in an `HttpOnly` cookie. No body payload is required.

### Mobile / Native Flow
Pass the `refresh_token` in the request body as JSON.

### Post-Request Script
Automatically updates the `access_token` and `refresh_token` variables in your Postman collection upon success.

# 📱 Get Active Sessions

Retrieve a list of all active login sessions and devices associated with the authenticated user.

variable.description: The unique session ID to revoke

request.description:
# 🚫 Revoke Session

Revoke a specific active session, forcing that device to log out.

### URL Parameters
*   `id`: The unique session ID (obtained from the `Get Active Sessions` response).

item.name: User Profile
item.description: Manage user account details, profile pictures, and settings.

request.description:
# 👤 Get Profile

Retrieve the authenticated user's profile information.

### Response Includes
*   Basic info (Name, Email, Phone)
*   Member ID
*   Profile Picture URL
*   Address
*   Favorite Pharmacies

# ✏️ Update Profile

Update personal information.

### Supports Partial Updates
You can send only the fields you want to change.

### Mutable Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `firstname` | string | First Name |
| `lastname` | string | Last Name |
| `email` | email | Must be unique. |
| `phone_number` | string | Must be unique. |
| `dob` | date | Date of Birth (YYYY-MM-DD). |
| `gender` | string | `male`, `female`, `other`, `prefer_not_to_say` |
| `gps_code` | string | Digital address (e.g., GA-183-2011) |
| `address` | string | Physical address. |

formdata.description: Image file (jpeg, png, jpg, gif, max 2MB)

request.description:
# 📸 Upload Picture

Set a profile picture for the first time.

### Requirements
*   **Format**: JPEG, PNG, JPG, GIF
*   **Max Size**: 2MB

# 🔄 Replace Picture

Replace the existing profile picture.

### Logic
Deletes the old image from storage before saving the new one.

query.description: Search query for products, generics (drugs), brands, and categories
Set to 1 to track this search. Logs query + match to recent_searches.

request.description:
# 🔍 Smart Hybrid Search

Advanced hybrid search across products, generic drugs, brands, and categories. Uses a tiered confidence scoring system to determine the most relevant result.

### 🎯 Confidence Tiers
1.  **Navigational (Exact Match)**: If query matches a category or brand name exactly, it returns a navigational top_match.
2.  **Transactional (Product Match)**: Search for specific combinations like "Panadol 500mg syrup".
3.  **Informational (Broad Match)**: Fallback search across names and descriptions.

### 📦 Structured Results
- `top_match`: The single best result for navigation (navigate vs results).
- `results.products`: Specific drug variants (e.g. Panadol Tablet 500mg).
- `results.generics`: Generic medication groups (e.g. Paracetamol).
- `results.brands`: Pharmaceutical brands.
- `results.categories`: Medical categories.

### 📊 Analytics Tracking
When `track=1` is passed, the server logs the search query and the resolved entity for personalized recommendations and trends.

# 📊 Track Search

Manually log a search query. Useful for client-side tracking when the `track=1` param on Smart Search is not being used.

### Body Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `query` | string | **Required**. The search text. |
| `match_type` | string | Optional. One of `drug`, `brand`, `category`, `product`. |
| `match_id` | integer | Optional. ID of the matched entity. |
| `user_id` | integer | Optional. Logged-in user ID (resolved server-side for authenticated requests). |

Filter by pharmacy name or location

request.description:
### 🏷️ Price Comparison

Returns exact pricing and availability for a specific drug variant across all pharmacies.

**Standardized Output**: Now uses the `PharmacyDrugPriceResource` format, providing a clean, flat object with product metadata and nested pharmacy details.

#### Mandatory Filter
While you can provide `drug_id`, `brand_id`, etc., it is highly recommended to pass a single `product_id` for the most accurate variant matching.

query.description: Current variant Brand ID (to exclude from related list)
Current variant Form ID (to exclude from related list)
Current variant Strength ID (to exclude from related list)
Current variant UOM ID (to exclude from related list)
Search by Brand Name or Form Name
Filter results by specific Brand ID
Filter results by specific Form ID
Filter results by specific Strength ID
Filter results by specific UOM ID

request.description: Returns pricing data for other variants (brand, form, strength) of the same drug, matching the exact format from the legacy related_drugs response, but fully paginated.
Get user's saved drugs. Supports pagination.
Public endpoint - requires API key. Returns globally recent drugs (or user-specific if authenticated). Supports pagination.
Public endpoint - requires API key. Get top searched drugs globally. Supports pagination.
Get user's recently viewed drugs. Supports pagination. Also accepts 'limit' parameter for backward compatibility.
Search and list pharmacies with optional geo-filtering.

# 🏥 Pharmacy Detail

Returns full pharmacy profile including services (as objects) and branches (with their own services, delivery, and payment fields).

### Services shape (in response)
```json
"services": [
  { "id": 1, "name": "Prescription Dispensing", "slug": "prescription-dispensing", "category": "dispensing", "description": "...", "icon": "..." }
]
```

### Branch shape
Each branch includes: `is_active`, `is_open`, `working_hours`, `languages`, `services`, `delivery` (available/radius_km/base_fee/fee_per_km), `accepted_payment_methods`.

Branch **null = inherit** from parent pharmacy for `working_hours`, `languages`, and payment methods.

# 🏪 Pharmacy Branches

Returns all branches for a pharmacy. Each branch includes:

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `services` | Branch-specific services; falls back to pharmacy services if branch has none |
| `working_hours` | Branch hours override; null = inherit from pharmacy |
| `languages` | Languages spoken at this branch; null = inherit from pharmacy |
| `delivery` | Branch-level delivery settings (`available`, `radius_km`, `base_fee`, `fee_per_km`) |
| `accepted_payment_methods` | `['platform', 'direct']`; null = inherit from pharmacy |
| `is_active` | Whether this branch is currently operational |
| `is_open` | Current open/closed status |
| `banner_image` | Branch-specific banner image URL |

# 🚚 Delivery Options

Returns available delivery methods for a pharmacy with estimated fees.

**Methods:** `pickup` (always available, fee 0) · `pharmacy_delivery` (if pharmacy delivers) · `fyndrx_delivery` (if platform delivery enabled)

Fee = base_fee + (distance_km × fee_per_km) when lat/lng are provided. Without coords, only base fee is returned.
Public endpoint - requires API key. Search pharmacies that have specific drugs. Pass drugs as JSON string in query parameter.
Highly filterable endpoint for pharmacy drug prices. Replaces the legacy /pharmacy-prices/drug/{id} logic with proper pagination and geolocation support.
Public endpoint - requires API key. Get all pharmacy prices. Supports pagination.
Public endpoint - requires API key. Get prices for a specific pharmacy. Supports pagination.

query.description: Optional: Filter by brand ID (integer — brands table)
Optional: Filter by form ID (integer — forms table)
Optional: Filter by strength ID (integer — strengths table). Previously called form_strength_id.
Optional: Filter by UOM ID (integer — uoms table). Previously called strength_uom_id.

request.description:
Get prices for a specific drug across all pharmacies. Returns `exact_match` (the variant matching the brand/form/strength/uom filters) and `related_drugs` (other available variants of the same drug).

**Filter params** — all optional, all use direct catalog table IDs:
- `brand_id` → integer ID from `brands` table
- `form_id` → integer ID from `forms` table
- `strength_id` → integer ID from `strengths` table
- `uom_id` → integer ID from `uoms` table

Filtering and grouping is done via the `product` relationship — no junction table IDs.

Distance sorting available when `lat` + `lng` are provided.

request.description: Public endpoint - requires API key. Get all drug forms. Supports pagination.

item.name: Products
item.description: Product SKU catalog. Products are the purchasable variants: Drug + Brand + Form + Strength + UOM. IDs are UUIDs.

request.description: List all active product SKUs with brand, drug, form, strength, and UOM details.

item.name: Get Product by UUID or Slug

request.description:
Retrieve a single product by UUID or slug.

**Accepts:**
- UUID: `019e9bb6-ba54-71a1-9de4-da6216739e01`
- Slug: `panadol-tablets-500mg`

**Note:** Integer IDs are no longer valid after the UUID migration.

item.name: Cart
item.description: Manage user shopping cart. Items are grouped by pharmacy.

request.description:
### 🛒 Fetch Shopping Cart

Retrieves the user's current shopping cart, grouped intelligently by pharmacy.

**Standardized Items**: Each cart item is now transformed via `PharmacyDrugPriceResource`, providing flattened product data (name, image, dosage) and ensuring snake_case consistency.

**✨ Payment Methods**: Each pharmacy group includes `accepted_payment_methods` (e.g. `['platform', 'direct']`). The frontend **MUST** use these to filter available payment options at checkout.

### Response Structure
```json
{
  "pharmacies": [
    {
      "pharmacy_id": 1,
      "pharmacy_name": "Medicare",
      "accepted_payment_methods": ["platform", "direct"],
      "items": [
        {
          "id": 10, 
          "cart_item_id": 1, 
          "name": "Panadol Tablet 500mg",
          "quantity": 2,
          "price": 10.50,
          ...
        }
      ],
      "subtotal": 21.00
    }
  ],
  "total": 21.00,
  "total_items": 2
}
```

# ➕ Add Item

Add a drug to the cart.

### Fields
- `pharmacy_drug_price_id`: **Required**. The ID of the specific price/stock listing from a pharmacy (not just the drug ID).
- `quantity`: **Required**. Min 1, Max 99.

### Logic
- If item exists, updates quantity (if allowed).
- Checks stock before adding.

# ✏️ Update Quantity

Update the quantity of a cart item.

# 🗑️ Remove Item

Remove a single item from the cart.

# 🧹 Clear Cart

Remove all items from the cart.

# 🔄 Sync Cart

Replace the server-side cart with a local cart (e.g., from Guest session).

### Warning
This **overwrites** existing cart items on the server.

item.name: Orders
item.description: Manage order creation, tracking, and history.

request.description:
### 📦 Create a New Order

Submits an order for processing.

**🛡️ Payment Enforcement Validation**:
This endpoint strictly enforces payment mode configurations set by the Super Admin. If you pass a `payment_method` (e.g., `platform` for online, `direct` for offline) that the specific pharmacy does *not* accept, the API will return a `422 Unprocessable Entity` with a clear error message indicating the accepted methods.

**Rate Limit**: `20 requests/minute` (api:orders throttle group).

# 📦 Create Order

Checkout items from the cart.

### Modes
1.  **Single Branch Checkout**: Provide `pharmacy_branch_id`. Only items from that branch are ordered.
2.  **Checkout All**: Omit `pharmacy_branch_id`. Creates multiple orders (one per branch).

### Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phone_number` | string | **Required**. |
| `delivery_method` | enum | `pickup` or `delivery`. |
| `payment_method` | enum | `platform` (online) or `direct` (cash/POS). |
| `delivery_address` | string | Required if method is `delivery`. |
| `delivery_methods` | array | Map of `branch_id` -> method (for multi-checkout). |
| `payment_methods` | array | Map of `branch_id` -> method (for multi-checkout). |

query.description: Optional filter: pending, confirmed, processing, ready, out_for_delivery, completed, cancelled

request.description:
# 📜 Order History

List all orders placed by the user.

### 📄 Order Details

Retrieves full details of a specific order using the standardized `OrderResource`.

**Schema Update**: All fields are now strictly `snake_case` (e.g., `order_number`, `pharmacy_name`, `delivery_fee`). The `items` array uses standardized drug metadata similar to the cart.

# 🚚 Track Order

Get tracking timeline.

### Response
- `current_step`: 1-6 integer.
- `total_steps`: 5 (Pickup) or 6 (Delivery).
- `status_history`: Array of status changes.

# 🚫 Cancel Order

Cancel a pending order.

### Restrictions
Cannot cancel if status is `completed` or `processed` (depending on policy).

# 📎 Upload Rx

Upload a prescription image/PDF for an order that requires it.
Creates a `Prescription` record linked to the order.

item.name: Prescriptions
item.description: Manage user prescriptions and associated drugs.

request.description:
# 📋 List Prescriptions

Get all prescriptions for the authenticated user.

### Response
Returns a list of prescriptions, ordered by latest created. Includes `drugs` count.

# ➕ Create Prescription

Create a new prescription record. This creates the "folder" to which you can add drugs later.

### Auto-Generation
If `prescription_number` is not provided, it is automatically generated (e.g., `RX-2024-1234`).

### Inputs
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `prescription_picture` | file | Image of the Rx. |
| `doctor_name` | string | Optional |
| `prescription_date` | date | Optional |
| `expiry_date` | date | Optional. Must be > prescription_date |
| `status` | string | `active` (default), `pending_approval`, `expired` |

# 📄 Prescription Details

Get full details including the list of drugs.

### Includes
*   `user` object
*   `drugs` array (with `drug`, `brand`, `form`, `uom` details)

# ✏️ Update Prescription

Update prescription details.

### Note on File Uploads
To update the picture, use `POST` with `_method: PUT` because standard PUT requests do not support `multipart/form-data` file uploads efficiently in some clients.

# 🗑️ Delete Prescription

Removes the prescription and its associated image.

# 💊 Add Single Drug

Add one medication to this prescription.

### Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `drug_id` | int | **Required**. ID of the generic drug. |
| `quantity` | int | **Required**. |
| `dose` | string | e.g. "500mg". |
| `brand_id` | int | Optional. Specific brand. |
| `form_id` | int | Optional. Tablet, Syrup, etc. |
| `frequency` | string | Optional. e.g. "1-0-1" |
| `duration` | string | Optional. e.g. "5 days" |

# 📦 Bulk Add Drugs

Add multiple medications at once.

### Usage
Useful when importing a full prescription.

### Body
Requires a `drugs` array containing objects with the same fields as the single add endpoint.

# 🔄 Bulk Update Drugs

Update existing drugs in the prescription.

### Requirement
Each object in the `drugs` array MUST include `prescription_drug_id` to identify which record to update.

# 📢 Request/Revoke

Toggle the `has_request` flag.

- `GET /request`: Sets `has_request = 1`
- `GET /revoke`: Sets `has_request = 0`

item.name: Reviews
item.description: User reviews for Pharmacies and Drugs.

query.description: Required: 'pharmacy', 'drug', or 'medication' (drug and medication are aliases)
Required: UUID for pharmacy, integer ID for drug

request.description:
# 🌟 Get Reviews

Fetch reviews for a specific entity (Pharmacy or Drug).

# ✍️ Write Review

Submit a new review.

### Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `reviewable_type` | string | **Required**. `pharmacy`, `drug`, or `medication` (drug/medication are aliases). |
| `reviewable_id` | string | **Required**. UUID for pharmacy; integer ID for drug. |
| `rating` | int | **Required**. 1-5. |
| `title` | string | **Required**. |
| `comment` | string | **Required**. |
| `order_id` | string | Optional. UUID of the order. Links review to a verified purchase. |

# ✏️ Update Review

Modify your existing review.

# 🗑️ Delete Review

Permanently remove your review.

# 👍 Mark Helpful

Vote on a review's helpfulness. Toggle `is_helpful` to true/false.

query.description: UUID for pharmacy, integer ID for drug

request.description:
# 📊 Review Stats

Get average rating and breakdown for an entity.

# 🗣️ Get Testimonies

Fetch a list of active testimonies.

item.name: Blog

request.description:
# 📰 List Posts

Get latest blog posts with pagination.

# 📄 Read Post

Get full content of a blog post by its slug.

# 🔗 Related Posts

Get similar posts based on category/tags.

# 📂 Categories

List available blog categories.

# 🏷️ Tags

List available tags.

# 💬 Get Comments

List comments for a post.

# ❤️ Like Post

Toggle like status for a post. Works for guests (IP-based) and auth users.

# 🗨️ Post Comment

Add a comment to a post.

### Guest vs Auth
- **Guests**: Must provide `name` and `email`.
- **Auth Users**: `name` and `email` are nullable (taken from profile).

item.name: Notifications
item.description: Manage user notifications.

item.name: Consultations
item.description: Full consultation lifecycle for patients and pharmacists.

formdata.description: Options: general, medication_review, chronic_disease, acute_illness, wellness, vaccination
Auto-filled from profile if omitted (authenticated users)
Optional: route to a specific pharmacy (UUID)
Optional: route to a specific branch
ISO datetime for a future appointment
Link this as a follow-up to a previous consultation ID

request.description:
## Create Consultation (Authenticated)

Start a new consultation. Status is always set to `pending` by the server.

### Patient-only fields
Clinical data (vitals, medical history, diagnoses, priority) is **staff-only** and cannot be set via this endpoint.

### Follow-up flow
To submit a follow-up to a previous consultation, include `parent_consultation_id`. The parent must belong to the same authenticated user.

query.description: general | medication_review | chronic_disease | acute_illness | wellness | vaccination
Search by ref number or patient info

request.description: List consultations for the authenticated user with optional filters.

Get full consultation details.

**Response includes:** `user`, `prescription`, `diagnoses`, `assignedPharmacist`, `parentConsultation` (if follow-up), and `followUpConsultations` list.

Update an open consultation (status must be `pending` or `in_review`).

### Patient-editable fields
- `chief_complaint`
- `consultation_notes`
- `scheduled_at`

Staff-only fields are rejected with 422.

body.raw:
{
  "pharmacist_notes": "Recommended rest and hydration. Take Paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours.",
  "recommendations": "Drink at least 2L of water daily. Return if fever persists beyond 3 days.",
  "requires_followup": true,
  "followup_date": "2026-06-16T09:00:00",
  "followup_notes": "Review BP and confirm symptoms have resolved."
}

request.description:
**Pharmacist / Admin role only.**

Submit a clinical response. Status changes to `responded`.

### Fields
| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `pharmacist_notes` | ✅ | Clinical observations |
| `recommendations` | — | Treatment plan |
| `requires_followup` | — | `true` / `false` |
| `followup_date` | — | ISO datetime, required when `requires_followup` is true |
| `followup_notes` | — | Instructions for the follow-up appointment |
| `followup_date` | — | ISO datetime |
Mark the consultation as `completed`. Can be called by the patient or assigned pharmacist.
Cancel a consultation. Only the patient or an admin can cancel.

Submit a star rating and optional comment after the consultation.

- Only the consultation owner (patient) can rate.
- `rating`: integer 1–5 (required)
- `feedback`: free text (optional)

Create a new consultation as a follow-up to an existing one.

Set `parent_consultation_id` to the ID of the original consultation. The parent consultation's **Follow-up Consultations** list in the admin panel will automatically show the new record.

**Requirements:**
- Must be authenticated
- The parent consultation must belong to the same user

**Response** includes `parent_consultation` object with the original ref number and status.
Soft-delete a consultation. Only the patient or an admin can delete.

Aggregated counts for the authenticated user's consultations.

**Returns:** total, pending, in_review, responded, completed, cancelled, requires_followup, average_rating, total_rated.
Filter consultations by type. Replace `general` in the path with any consultation type slug.

item.name: Stats & Analytics
item.description: Platform statistics and analytics.

request.description: Stats for the last 24 hours.

### 💳 Initialize Online Payment

Generates a Paystack checkout URL for the user to complete their transaction.

**🔒 Security Guard**:
Before initializing, this endpoint verifies the target pharmacy's `accepts_online_payment` flag. If the pharmacy has disabled online payments since the order was created, it returns a `403 Forbidden` response preventing the transaction.

**Rate Limit**: `15 requests/minute` (api:payments throttle group - heavily rate-limited for security).

Initialize Paystack transaction for an order.
Verify Paystack transaction by reference.

item.name: Favorites

request.description:
Returns drugs and/or pharmacies in a single response.

`type` param:
- `all` — both sections (default)
- `drugs` — drug favourites only
- `pharmacies` — pharmacy favourites only
Returns `{ drugs, pharmacies, total }` counts.

Paginated list of saved drug / product favourites.

Each item includes a `type` field (`product` or `drug`) and either a `product` or `drug` object with full details.

Save a drug or product variant to favourites.

### Fields
| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `product_id` | **Preferred.** Saves a specific product variant (drug + brand + form + strength). |
| `drug_id` | Saves a generic drug. Required if `product_id` is omitted. |
| `brand_id` | Optional variant refinement when using `drug_id`. |
| `form_id` | Optional. |
| `strength_id` | Optional. |
| `qty` | Default: 1. |

**Returns 200** if already favourited, **201** if newly added.
Remove a drug from favourites. The `{id}` param is matched against `id`, `drug_id`, or `product_id` — whichever resolves first.
Returns `{ id, is_favourited: bool }`. The ID is matched against both `drug_id` and `product_id`.

Add multiple favourites in one request. Returns `{ added, skipped }` counts.

Each item follows the same `product_id` / `drug_id` rules as the single-add endpoint. Already-saved items are silently skipped.

Remove multiple drug favourites at once.

`ids` is matched against `id`, `drug_id`, and `product_id` — any match is removed.

> **Note:** Uses `POST` (not `DELETE`) to avoid HTTP client issues with request bodies on DELETE requests.
Paginated list of saved pharmacies.
Save a pharmacy to favourites. Returns 200 if already saved, 201 if new.
Remove a pharmacy from favourites.
Returns `{ pharmacy_id, is_favourited: bool }`.

item.description: Save and manage a user's favourite drugs (by product variant or generic drug) and pharmacies.

item.name: User Activity
item.description: Recent searches and recently viewed items.

request.description:
# 🔎 Recent Search History

Returns the authenticated user's last 10 search queries. Useful for displaying a "Recent Searches" list in the UI.

# 🏆 Top Searches

Returns the most popular search queries across the entire platform, aggregated by unique query text.

# 🗂️ Store Recent Search (Legacy)

Legacy GET endpoint to log a drug search. Internally now writes to the new `recent_searches` table with `match_type = 'drug'` and `match_id = drugId`. Use the Smart Search `track=1` param or POST `/search/track` for new integrations.

item.name: Stats & Analytics
item.description: Platform-wide statistics and analytics. All endpoints require an API key. Support `period` filter (today, week, month, year, last_7_days, last_30_days, last_90_days, all) or custom `from`/`to` date range (YYYY-MM-DD).

request.description:
# 📊 Platform Overview

High-level metrics: users, orders, revenue, searches, views, consultations.

### Search Metrics (Updated Schema)
The `searches` object now reports:
- `total` - SUM of `search_count` across all match types
- `unique_drugs` - Count of distinct `match_id` where `match_type IN (drug, product)`

# 🔎 Search Analytics

Detailed breakdown of search activity.

### Updated Response Fields
- `top_searches` - Aggregated by **query text** (not drug_id), reflecting actual user search terms.
- `trending_drugs` - Computed by comparing recent vs. previous 7-day search volume for `match_type IN (drug, product)`.
- `recent_searches` - Returns last N records, each with `query`, `match_type`, `match_id`, and resolved `drug_name`.

# 👤 User Behavior

User engagement metrics: searches per user, most active users, session patterns.

# 💰 Revenue Stats

Order revenue breakdown by period, payment method, and pharmacy.

# 📝 Content Stats

Blog post views, most viewed drugs, and content engagement metrics.

# 🏥 Pharmacy Performance

Rankings by orders, revenue, and drug availability across all registered pharmacies.

# 💊 Drug Performance

Top searched and top ordered drugs. Search ranking is aggregated from `recent_searches` filtered by `match_type IN (drug, product)` and grouped by `match_id`.

# ⚡ Real-Time Stats

Live metrics for the last 24 hours: active users, orders in progress, searches, and unique drugs searched (scoped to `match_type IN (drug, product)`).

item.name: Consultations
item.description: Public consultation endpoints (no authentication required).

request.description:
Create a consultation without authentication.

**Required for guests:** `patient_name`, `patient_email`, `patient_phone`

**Optional:** `consultation_type`, `chief_complaint`, `pharmacy_id`, `scheduled_at`, `source`

**Not allowed:** vitals, medical_history, allergies, status, priority — these are staff-only fields.

Authenticated users can omit patient fields (auto-filled from profile) and may pass `parent_consultation_id` to link as a follow-up.

query.description: Phone number for identity verification (or use patient_email)
Email for identity verification (alternative to phone)

request.description:
Look up a consultation by its reference number without needing to log in. Useful for the 'Track my consultation' public page.

**Security:** Either `patient_phone` or `patient_email` must match the record. Returns a generic 404 on any mismatch to prevent enumeration.

item.name: Pharmacy Applications
item.description: Pharmacy onboarding application lifecycle. All IDs are UUIDs.

request.description:
Start a new pharmacy onboarding application. Returns a UUID `application_id` — save it and use it for all subsequent steps.

The test script above automatically saves the returned `application_id` as `{{pharmacy_application_id}}`.
Retrieve the full application record by UUID. Returns all fields including current status, step, and uploaded documents.

Update application progress. Only allowed when status is `draft` or `rejected`.

Send any subset of fields — the server merges them. Advance `step` as the user progresses through the form.

**Returns 422** if status is not `draft` or `rejected`.
**Returns 404** if application UUID does not exist.

formdata.description: gpc_license | fda_certificate | business_registration | tin_certificate | superintendent_license | facility_exterior | facility_interior | proof_of_address | logo | banner_image

request.description: Upload a verification document or brand asset. Replaces any existing file of the same type.

Submit the completed draft for admin review. Status moves from `draft` → `pending`.

All required fields and documents must be uploaded before submission. Returns 422 with the missing field name if incomplete.

Resubmit a previously rejected application after the applicant has resolved the issues. Status moves to `resubmitted`.

Only allowed when status is `rejected`.
